# Correlation rule — mass file write / ransomware (T1486)

**Suggested severity**: Critical · **Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Scheduled correlation rule: one process touching >100 distinct files in a 5-minute window — the bulk-encryption signature of ransomware impact. `count_distinct(action_file_path) as files_touched` is the blast-radius metric; `count_distinct(action_file_extension) as exts` helps spot a single new extension across writes (encryption marker). Scope with `event_type = ENUM.FILE` only (event_sub_type is not reliably queryable). Critical severity (auto-opens a case immediately). Tune the threshold to your backup/indexing baseline; a burst straddling a bin boundary can split across two windows, so consider a lower threshold or overlapping windows for high-fidelity.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_image_sha256, action_file_path, action_file_extension
| bin _time span = 5m
| comp count_distinct(action_file_path) as files_touched, count() as ops, count_distinct(action_file_extension) as exts by _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_image_sha256
| filter files_touched > 100
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_image_sha256, files_touched, ops, exts
```

_Correlation-rule XQL body live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (runs to SUCCESS with confirmed fields). The schedule, time window, threshold-tuning, severity, MITRE mapping and suppression are rule-wrapper settings in the XSIAM editor — not in the XQL. See the `cortex_detection_rule_authoring` skill._
